title: Merge, Reshape, and Aggregate Multi-Source Data
tagline: Combine 3 CSVs with different schemas into one clean analysis-ready DataFrame.
scenario: A retail chain stores its data across three separate systems: `customers.csv` (customer demographics), `orders.csv` (order headers), and `order_items.csv` (line items with product and price). Your analyst needs a single, denormalized DataFrame to work from.
brief: Write the pandas code to load, merge, reshape, and aggregate these three files into one clean DataFrame. Handle schema mismatches (different column names, date formats, missing keys).
deliverables: The complete Python script showing the merge strategy (inner, left, etc.) and join keys
How you handled schema mismatches (column renames, type casting, date parsing)
The final aggregated output: revenue per customer, with at least 2 derived columns
submissionGuidance: Show that you understand the difference between inner, left, and outer joins. A wrong join type silently drops data.

rubric.label: Merge Strategy
rubric.description: Are the join types and keys chosen correctly to avoid data loss?

rubric.label: Schema Handling
rubric.description: Are column renames, type casts, and null handling done correctly?

rubric.label: Aggregation Quality
rubric.description: Are the derived columns meaningful and the groupby operations correct?
